title: Multiply by 0 or 1
description: Multiply 0 or 1 times a number less than or equal to 10.

hints:
We can think of $\blueD{1} \times \greenD{4}$ as $\blueD1$ group of $\greenD4$ circles:



[[Ōśā image 1]]

How many circles are there?

$\begin{align}&\phantom{\Large{\times}}\Large\blueD1\\
\\
&\underline{\Large\times\Large\greenD4}\\
\\
&\phantom{\Large{\times}}\Large4
\end{align}$

We can think of $\blueD{1} \times \greenD{1}$ as $\blueD1$ group of $\greenD1$ circle:

[[Ōśā image 1]]

How many circles are there?

$\begin{align}&\phantom{\Large{\times}}\Large\blueD1\\
\\
&\underline{\Large\times\Large\greenD1}\\
\\
&\phantom{\Large{\times}}\Large1
\end{align}$

\\
&\underline{\Large\times\Large\greenD1}\\
\\
&\phantom{\Large{\times}}\Large9
\end{align}$

We can think of $\blueD{1} \times \greenD{8}$ as $\blueD1$ group of $\greenD8$ circles:

We can think of $\blueD{1} \times \greenD{2}$ as $\blueD1$ group of $\greenD2$ circles:


[[Ōśā image 1]]

How many circles are there?
$\Large{\blueD{1} \times \greenD{2} = {2}}$

We can think of $\blueD{1} \times \greenD{6}$ as $\blueD1$ group of $\greenD6$ circles:

We can think of $\blueD{3} \times \greenD{1}$ as $\blueD3$ groups of $\greenD1$ circle:

We can think of $\blueD{1} \times \greenD{10}$ as $\blueD1$ group of $\greenD{10}$ circles:

[[Ōśā image 2]]

How many circles are there?
$\Large{\blueD{1} \times \greenD{10} = {10}}$
We can think of $\blueD{0} \times \greenD{1}$ as $\blueD0$ groups of $\greenD1$.
Since there are $\blueD0$ groups, the answer is $0$.

$\begin{align}&\phantom{\Large{\times}}\Large\blueD0\\
\\
&\underline{\Large\times\Large\greenD1}\\
\\
&\phantom{\Large{\times}}\Large0
\end{align}$
We can think of $\blueD{0} \times \greenD{4}$ as $\blueD0$ groups of $\greenD4$.
Since there are $\blueD0$ groups, the answer is $0$.

\\
&\underline{\Large\times\Large\greenD4}\\
\\
&\phantom{\Large{\times}}\Large0
\end{align}$

[[Ōśā image 1]]

&\phantom{\Large{\times}}\Large0
\end{align}$
